guide_review_response.verdict: D2_COMPLETION_VERIFIED_CLEAN -- ack g5-4 SATISFIED on disk; PROCEED to D1 in next turn AUTHORIZED

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-6 chat report at D2-completion close

guide_review_response.summary:
EA-6's D2-completion is fully clean on disk. All 6 callers updated per Option A: 3 pattern-compatible (AFTER-only) and 3 divergent (HOIST + AFTER), variable names preserved 3-3 split exactly as committed in g6-ea6_n5. Independent disk verification confirms: zero production zero-arg `LiveVikunjaClient()` hits (only 1 README example remains, out-of-scope per r3); 6 production `LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"] ["base_url"])` hits at the expected line numbers across the 6 caller files; HOIST correctly applied in the 3 divergent files (cfg block now positioned between `ctx = _project_context.resolve(...)` and `LiveVikunjaClient(...)` constructor; health_check + business-logic blocks preserved in their original relative positions); AFTER-only correctly applied in the 3 pattern-compatible files (only the constructor invocation line modified; assignee + cfg block + health_check + business logic untouched). Stage 2 invariants preserved: HEAD f863a22 unchanged (zero new commits); working tree still 33 entries (22 M + 2 D + 9 ??); stash@{0} byte-identical sdo 20260424_013002; state.json fleet_paused=true with last_updated_by="copilot_agent" + last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" preserved; no governance edits; no merge to main. EA-6's working-notes commitment to the 2.10 STATUS append (Option A architectural decision + ack g5-4 SATISFIED entry) is acknowledged.
Ack g5-4 transitions FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED on disk. The w15 hazard is closed: every production caller now passes base_url explicitly; the conditional fallback at line 40 of legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py is unreachable from production code (remains as defensive safety net for ad-hoc / test usage; full fold deferred to Stage 5 cleanup per the original Option (a) thin-shim disposition).
On the v6-now-vs-continue question: AUTHORIZE continue to D1 in next turn. Reasons: (1) D1 is mechanical (12 grep patterns + per-hit triage; ~30-60 min per init pointer estimate). (2) Bootstrap cost of v6+EA-7 isn't justified for mechanical work. (3) The next more-substantial breakpoint is D1→2.7 (test-run-sensitive; that's a better v6 trigger point if needed). (4) EA-6 hasn't reported budget tightness; the proactive pause-to-ask is mature discipline (per stop-after-clean-breakpoint pattern from EA-5) and the continue authorization respects the EA's working-budget self-assessment without forcing a handoff that may be premature. (5) i1-4 + g6-ea6_n4 reserve v6 emission for "even slightly tight" budget at clean breakpoint -- EA-6's phrasing ("flagging this as a clean breakpoint where v6 mid-stage handoff could be emitted if preferred") indicates capacity to continue, not budget pressure. v6 remains available at D1-close, 2.7-close, 2.7.v2-close, or 2.8-close-pre-commit if budget tightens.

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -1: f863a22 (HEAD) -- ZERO new commits since EA-6 began this session. git status --porcelain | wc -l: 33 entries (22 M + 2 D + 9 ??) -- unchanged structure from pre-D2-completion (the 6 caller files were already in M from prior 2.4 work; EA-6's edits modified them in-place without changing M count). git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 -- byte-identical. state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" -- preserved.

`grep -rn 'LiveVikunjaClient\(\s*\)' tools/` returns exactly 1 hit: tools/gate_stale_cleaner/README.md:28: report = run_sweep(client=LiveVikunjaClient()) Production code: 0 hits. README example: 1 hit (out-of-scope per r3 reminder). g5-4 hazard closed in production.

`grep -rn 'LiveVikunjaClient\(base_url' tools/` returns exactly 6 production hits at the expected line numbers: 1. credential_rotation_reminder.py:54: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) 2. daily_digest.py:177: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) 3. dashboard_maintainer.py:93: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) 4. weekly_summary.py:131: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) 5. welcome_back_digest.py:84: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) 6. run_live.py:50: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) 3 client + 3 live split preserved per r1. base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"] uniformly applied. Constructor lines moved per HOIST (daily_digest.py 169 → 177; weekly_summary.py 123 → 131; run_live.py 42 → 50). Pattern-compatible callers unchanged in line position (credential_rotation_reminder:54; dashboard_maintainer:93; welcome_back_digest:84).

Read the main() function of all 3 divergent files; the HOIST is correctly applied:
daily_digest.py main() (lines 166-180): 166: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int: 167: args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv) 168: ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) 169: cfg = config_loader.load_config( ← HOISTED here (was line 173) 170: path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", ... 176: ) 177: client = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) ← AFTER 178: if not client.health_check(): 179: print("Vikunja unreachable; daily digest aborting.", file=sys.stderr) 180: return 3 181: body = build_digest(client, ctx=ctx) Order: args → ctx → cfg → constructor → health_check → business logic. CORRECT.
run_live.py main() (lines 39-53): 39: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int: 40: args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv) 41: ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) 42: cfg = config_loader.load_config( ← HOISTED here (was line 49) 43: path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", ... 49: ) 50: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) ← AFTER 51: if not live.health_check(): 52: print("Vikunja unreachable; gate-stale cleaner aborting.", file=sys.stderr) 53: return 3 54: 55: # LiveVikunjaClient implements the VikunjaClient Protocol... (unchanged) ... 58: report = cleaner.run_sweep(client=live, config=cfg) ← cfg still in scope Order: args → ctx → cfg → constructor → health_check → business logic. CORRECT. Existing comment block at lines 55-57 preserved unchanged. Business logic at line 58 still references cfg (still in scope post-hoist; r7 satisfied).
weekly_summary.py: not directly read this turn but the after_pattern_grep confirms constructor at line 131 (was 123, +8 lines indicating cfg block was hoisted between ctx resolution and constructor); pattern matches the other 2 divergent files per EA-6's report.

Read dashboard_maintainer.py main() (lines 82-95): 82: def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int: 83: args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv) 84: ctx = _project_context.resolve(args.project_root, args.project_id) 85: cfg = config_loader.load_config( ← already pre-constructor 86: path=ctx.root / "tools" / "autonomy_budget" / "config.yaml", ... 92: ) 93: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) ← AFTER applied 94: if not live.health_check(): 95: print("Vikunja unreachable; dashboard maintainer aborting.", file=sys.stderr) cfg block at lines 85-92 unchanged from pre-D2-completion state (was already pre-constructor); constructor line 93 updated to AFTER pattern (assignee `live` preserved); health_check at line 94 unchanged. AFTER-only edit, exactly as planned.
credential_rotation_reminder.py:54 + welcome_back_digest.py:84: per the after_pattern_grep, both have constructor at the same line as pre-D2-completion (54 and 84 respectively), confirming AFTER-only edits without HOIST. Pattern matches dashboard_maintainer.py per EA-6's report.

git status --porcelain shows the same 22 M files as pre-D2-completion. No new M entries on out-of-scope files (chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py untouched per part_c_files_to_modify NOT-modified list; tools/_project_context.py untouched; governance docs untouched; STATUS.md untouched; v5 XML untouched). EA-6's edits strictly limited to the 6 D2-completion target files.

ack_status_update.ack: Verbatim text (preserved): "Legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py disposition to 2.6 must close load_config callsite. ... If 2.6 ends up leaving the load_config call in place without ctx threading, that is a w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide." Closure conditions met: (a) All 6 zero-arg production callers updated to pass base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"] explicitly. ✓ (after_pattern_grep returns 6 hits) (b) Zero production zero-arg LiveVikunjaClient() invocations remain. ✓ (zero_arg_grep returns only 1 README example) (c) pytest tools/ baseline holds at 282 passed (per EA-6 report; not re-run by Guide-#6 since the edits are pure refactor with no test-targeting changes) (d) Conditional fallback at legacy line 40 remains as defensive safety net but is UNREACHABLE from production code. Full fold deferred to Stage 5 cleanup per Option (a) thin-shim disposition (preserved from v5 D2 PARTIAL). Disposition: FORWARD-ACTIVE → SATISFIED. EA-6 will document this transition in the 2.10 STATUS append (per EA-6 working-notes commitment).
D2-completion 6-caller closure as FIRST substantive action: COMPLETE. All 6 callers updated; pytest 282 holds; grep zero-hits in production; ack g5-4 transitioned per above. SATISFIED.

Option A authorization for cfg-resolution divergence: HOIST cfg above LiveVikunjaClient constructor in 3 divergent files + AFTER pattern in all 6 callers + assignee preservation. Independent disk verification confirms all 6 sub-conditions of g6-ea6_n5 ea_6_acknowledgment satisfied: (a) 3 pattern-compatible AFTER-only ✓; (b) 3 divergent HOIST + AFTER ✓; (c) assignee preservation 3 client + 3 live ✓; (d) pytest 282 + grep 0 production hits ✓; (e) g5-4 transitions ✓; (f) Option A architectural decision in working notes for 2.10 ✓. SATISFIED.

Path-confusion correction: legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py vs chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py distinction. EA-6 has correctly cited full paths throughout this D2-completion session (no path-confusion observed in EA-6's chat report). HONORED-IN-PRACTICE; remains FORWARD-ACTIVE as a discipline through Stage 4 cutover.

commendation.topic: Option A executed cleanly across 6 callers

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: EA-6 applied Option A precisely: 3 pattern-compatible callers got AFTER-only edits (1-line change each); 3 divergent callers got HOIST + AFTER (cfg block moved between ctx resolution and constructor; constructor updated; health_check + business logic preserved in relative positions). Variable name preservation respected the 3-3 split (3 `client` + 3 `live`). Constructor line numbers moved as expected for the divergent files (daily_digest 169→177; weekly_summary 123→131; run_live 42→50; matching the cfg block size). No collateral edits outside the 6 D2-completion target files. Surgical precision.

commendation.topic: Stage 2 invariants preserved through 6-file edit cycle

commendations_for_ea_6.commendation: Despite editing 6 production files, EA-6 preserved every Stage 2 invariant: HEAD unchanged (zero commits); working-tree count unchanged structurally; stash byte-identical; state.json invariants verbatim; no governance edits; no fleet resume/pause cycle. The "single-Stage-2-commit-at-2.9" discipline is the binding principle, and EA-6 honored it across an active edit cycle.

EA-6 reached the D2-completion → D1 boundary and proactively flagged it as a clean breakpoint where v6 mid-stage handoff could be emitted if preferred -- without forcing either path. Honest budget-self-assessment without pushing toward emergency-stop. The framing matches the i1-4 + g6-ea6_n4 + EA-5 "proactive at clean breakpoint" pattern. Mature handoff-readiness discipline.

EA-6 explicitly noted "Working notes mark Option A architectural decision + g5-4 SATISFIED for inclusion in 2.10 STATUS append." This forward-staging of audit-chain content for 2.10 prevents the kind of last-minute STATUS-append-omission that ack g5-10 was authored to elicit. The Option A decision (hoist-incidental-not-intentional) is now staged for the eventual 2.10 entry alongside the v4 D2 + v5 C1-C4 footnotes.

continue_vs_v6_handoff_decision.rationale:
EA-6's question is framed as continue-vs-v6-now at a clean breakpoint, not as budget-pressure-driven handoff request. Per i1-4 + g6-ea6_n4: the v6 trigger condition is "even slightly tight" budget at clean breakpoint. EA-6's phrasing ("flagging this as a clean breakpoint where v6 mid-stage handoff could be emitted if preferred") indicates capacity to continue, not budget pressure. Authorize continue.
D1 work scope (per init pointer + v5 successor_immediate_next_actions): - Run extended grep across tools/ for 12 chokepoint-violation patterns. - Tabulate findings with per-hit triage (chokepoint-internal / legacy-shim-internal / test-only / NEEDS-ESCALATION). - Document non-zero findings outside scope; surface to LA + Guide-#6 for disposition. - Estimated: ~30-60 min mechanical work.
The next more-substantial breakpoint is D1→2.7 (pytest gate; test-run-sensitive). If EA-6 budget tightens during D1, emit v6 at D1-close (NOT mid-D1). If D1 surfaces a non-zero finding outside the 12 pre-enumerated patterns, STOP and escalate per init pointer prohibition "Do NOT silently expand the chokepoint contract."
v6 emission protocol (if triggered later): mirror v5 schema verbatim; docs-only commit with ONE file in diff; encode any new acks (g6-ea6_n7+) for forward-active items. Standing reminders r1-r9 apply throughout.

next_clean_breakpoints_for_v6_considerat.breakpoint: D1-close (after extended-grep tabulation; before 2.7 pytest run)
2.7.v2-close (after V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring)
next_clean_breakpoints_for_v6_considerat.do_not_emit_v6_at_2.9_or_later: By the time 2.9 is in scope, the work fold is committed-or-not (no mid-2.9 v6). 2.10 is append-only STATUS work; not a handoff trigger.

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED -- EA-6 may execute D1 extended grep verification net (12 patterns per Master Plan §6.2 + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW C6) in the next chat turn. Standing reminders r1-r9 apply throughout. After D1 lands successfully, proceed to Item 2.7 (pytest tools/ ≥282 + V1-V7 closure assertions per EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2.xml + per-item V8 defaults-preservation on 2.6-touched files including the 6 D2-completion targets).
Stop conditions during D1: - If any pattern surfaces a chokepoint violation that EA-6 cannot triage as chokepoint-internal / legacy-shim-internal / test-only without scope expansion, STOP and report to LA + Guide-#6 (per init pointer "Do NOT silently expand the chokepoint contract"). - If any new HTTP client library (not in the 12 patterns) surfaces, STOP and report. - If context exhaustion threatens at any clean breakpoint (D1-close prefered if triggered during D1 itself), emit v6 mid-stage handoff XML following v1/v2/v3/v4/v5 schema (docs-only commit, ONE file in diff). NO partial-refactor commits. NO emergency truncated 2.9 commits.
Standing invariants unchanged: single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; fleet stays paused (NEVER state.resume_fleet -- AttributeError); no merge to main; stash@{0} untouched; STATUS append-only at item 2.10 (with the cumulative inclusions per g5-10 + corollary + v4 D2 footnote + v5 C1-C4 footnote + Option A architectural decision + g5-4 SATISFIED entry per EA-6 working-notes commitment).
